text:
In a quaint village nestled between rolling hills and whispering woods, there roamed a wise old owl named Old Harry. Every evening after the last moonbeam faded into twilight, he perched high in a great oak tree at the village's edge.

One late summer afternoon, a small rabbit chuffed its way through the underbrush. Its fur was dull green, and you couldn't quite make out its face. The rabbit spotted Old Harry perched there, looking larger than life.

With a chuckle that shook the ancient oak leaves, Old Harry called out, "Well met my furry friend!"

The bunny jumped into a small clearing. "Whoops! Didn't see that old owl!"

Old Harry hooted softly. "Well, well, what have we here? A visitor from far away. Care to share some grub?"

The rabbit scuffed its tiny nose. "Sure! I could eat a whole lot of those big owl pellets."

"A pellet?" Old Harry hooted skept
